guide.sub: Four decades of back-road stories, gathered into one trip through the gateway to the Smokies — craft studios in the Glades, breakfast on the Parkway, and the neighbors up the road in Pigeon Forge and Sevierville.
guide.metaNote: every one a real Crossroads segment
guide.mapLede: Every stop our cameras visited, plotted from the national park to Sevierville.
guide.quote: “Come travel the highways and by-ways of Tennessee.”
guide.note: We hope this guide leads you to new memories, a hidden gem, or someplace you’ve always meant to visit. Thanks for making Tennessee Crossroads part of your journey.
guide.colophonHTML: Tennessee Crossroads is a Nashville PBS production · Each place began as a story on the show

sections.title: Eat & Drink
sections.intro: An early mountain breakfast and a craft sandwich worth the drive — fuel up before a day in the Smokies.

stops.name: Crockett's Breakfast Camp
stops.blurb: Joe Elmore rises early in Gatlinburg to catch breakfast at Crockett's Breakfast Camp — a hearty mountain start to a day on the Parkway.

stops.name: Tennessee Jed's
stops.blurb: Great sandwiches in the Great Smoky Mountains: Miranda Cohen stops at Tennessee Jed's on Gatlinburg's Parkway, where craft sandwiches are built for a trail-day appetite.

sections.title: See & Explore
sections.intro: A skybridge above the treetops, the Titanic in the foothills, and 17,000 shakers — the Smokies corridor collects one-of-a-kinds.

stops.name: Gatlinburg SkyBridge
stops.blurb: You've probably heard about the Gatlinburg SkyBridge — Joe Elmore lets you experience it. The Smoky Mountain marvel gave the whole town a boost as it rebuilt from the 2016 fires.

stops.name: Salt & Pepper Shaker Museum
stops.blurb: About 17,000 salt and pepper shakers fill this one-of-a-kind Gatlinburg museum. Rob Wilds discovers there's more history on the dinner table than you ever imagined.

stops.name: The Old Mill Square
stops.blurb: Pigeon Forge history goes back further than the amusement parks and outlet malls — and at the Old Mill Square, you can experience it and even take some of it home. Joe Elmore pays a visit.

stops.name: Titanic Museum
stops.blurb: In Pigeon Forge, the Titanic Museum captures the passengers' experiences aboard the ill-fated ship, complete with recreations and interactive features. Joe Elmore steps aboard.

stops.name: Tennessee Museum of Aviation
stops.blurb: Tennesseans have played a big part in the history of aviation, and Sevierville's Tennessee Museum of Aviation puts those accomplishments on display. Rob Wilds takes the tour.

stops.name: Smoky Mountain Winterfest
stops.blurb: Fix that Christmas-is-over letdown: through the winter, Smoky Mountain Winterfest keeps the holiday season's glittering lights shining across Gatlinburg and its neighbors. Rob Wilds finds a bright getaway. Seasonal.

sections.title: Stay a While
sections.intro: A lodge you can only reach on foot and an inn named for its view — beds worth the trip themselves.

stops.name: Mt. LeConte Lodge
stops.blurb: The only way up is on foot — and the supplies ride in with the llamas. Will Pedigo joins the friends who make an annual trek to the lodge atop Mt. LeConte.

stops.name: Hippensteal's Mountain View Inn
stops.blurb: The name is a promise kept. Laura Faber enjoys the view at Hippensteal's Mountain View Inn, tucked into the hills between Gatlinburg and Sevierville.

sections.title: Makers
sections.intro: The country's largest independent community of artisans calls these hills home — studios, schools, and workshops where craft is a way of life.

stops.name: Great Smoky Arts & Crafts Community
stops.blurb: Laura Faber explores the Great Smoky Arts & Crafts Community, the largest independent community of artisans and craftspeople in the country — a loop of working studios and shops along Glades Road.

stops.name: Arrowmont School of Arts and Crafts
stops.blurb: Just off Gatlinburg's busy Parkway hides an artistic oasis overflowing with creativity. Joe Elmore makes a return visit to Arrowmont School of Arts and Crafts.

stops.name: Dinwoodie Metal Sculptors
stops.blurb: A couple who share a love for each other — and a love for creating art from copper. Tressa Bush meets the Dinwoodies and watches metal turn into art.

stops.name: Michael Copas, Woodcarver
stops.blurb: Where most of us see a fallen tree, Michael Copas sees what's waiting inside. Miranda Cohen watches the master woodcarver turn trees into art.
